tldr: These tools solve different problems — Fyne is a Go GUI toolkit, Hyprland is a Linux Wayland compositor — so this is a workflow shift, not a code port.
why_switch: You want a tiling, keyboard-driven Linux desktop rather than building cross-platform GUI apps
Hyprland gives a customizable Wayland environment with smooth animations
Strong plugin ecosystem for window management workflows
what_to_export: List of Fyne apps you currently ship or maintain
Keyboard shortcut conventions used inside your apps
User preferences/config files your apps read
Documentation pointing users at the apps
export_method: Treat this as two separate concerns. For Fyne: keep the Go source under version control and document build commands so the apps remain maintainable. For Hyprland: there is nothing to export from Fyne into Hyprland — instead capture your current desktop workflow (shortcuts, app launchers, monitor layout) as notes you will reproduce in hyprland.conf. Consult the official Hyprland wiki for current configuration syntax.
import_method: Install Hyprland on a supported Linux distribution following the official Hyprland wiki. Build a hyprland.conf with monitor, input, keybind, and animation sections. Pair Hyprland with the launcher, bar, and notification daemon of your choice. Your existing Fyne apps continue to run as Wayland or XWayland clients — no code change needed unless you hit toolkit-specific Wayland bugs.

concept_mapping.note: Hyprland tiles or floats it like any other app
Hyprland does not theme app internals

gotchas: Hyprland is not a GUI toolkit; you cannot port Fyne app code to it
Some Go/Fyne apps may behave differently under Wayland — test XWayland fallback
Hyprland config syntax changes between versions; pin a release
Fractional scaling and HiDPI need explicit configuration
Screen sharing and color management depend on portals being installed

email.subject: Switching my Linux desktop to Hyprland
email.body:
Hey,

Quick note: I'm moving my Linux workstation to Hyprland for a tiling, keyboard-driven workflow. This is independent of the Fyne apps we ship — those keep building from the same Go source and run as Wayland clients on Hyprland.

I'll capture my hyprland.conf and shortcut cheat sheet in the team wiki so anyone can replicate the setup.

Thanks
